title: Compound V job_result
description: Canonical, backend-independent result of one dispatched job. Identical shape across the claude, codex, and antigravity adapters. The enforcement fields (blocked, files_changed, violations) are GIT-DERIVED by the caller, never self-reported by the worker model; the model's --output-last-message text feeds only `summary`. Also usable as a codex `--output-schema` target.

status.description: Terminal disposition of the job. `blocked` means a scope violation; `timeout` means the timeout wrapper fired; `error` is any other non-zero exit.

blocked.description: True if any file outside write_allowed changed. Git-derived by the scope gate, never model-self-reported. When true, status MUST be `blocked` and the caller MUST NOT merge.

files_changed.description: Repo-relative paths the job actually changed: `git diff --name-only` UNION `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` inside the worktree (or against the baseline commit for direct jobs).

violations.description: Subset of files_changed that fall outside write_allowed. Non-empty ⇒ blocked. Git-derived.

summary.description: Human-readable one-paragraph summary. Sourced from --output-last-message (or the schema'd JSON) — informational only, never used for enforcement.

session_id.description: Backend session/run identifier for resume. For codex: the UUID captured from the first `--json` `thread.started` event's `thread_id` (UUID-validated), resumed via `codex exec resume <uuid>` only under the resume-eligibility rule. Empty string when the backend has no resumable session.

worktree.description: Absolute path of the worktree the job ran in, or empty string for `direct` (in-place) jobs. Worktrees live under $TMPDIR/compound-v/<run-id>/<job-id>.

exit_code.description: Raw process exit code of the worker (0 on success; 124 if the timeout wrapper fired).

failure_class.description: On a non-success status, the classified backend-failure reason from compound-v-classify-failure.py (out_of_credits | rate_limited | overloaded | auth | context_length | timeout | network | other). null on success/blocked. Drives the dispatcher's deterministic retry/reroute/halt policy (compound-v-failure-policy.py): out_of_credits/auth never retry; out_of_credits circuit-breaks the backend and re-routes remaining jobs to the fallback; transient classes retry with capped backoff.

retry_after_seconds.description: Seconds-until-retry parsed from the provider message (HTTP Retry-After, or a codex usage-limit countdown like 'try again in 5 days'), 0 when unknown. The dispatcher passes this to compound-v-failure-policy.py --retry-after so a retry honors the provider's stated wait instead of synthetic backoff.
